product_desc: Cloud-native monitoring and security platform providing infrastructure monitoring, APM, log management, and real-user monitoring
customer_segment: Series B SaaS companies (50-200 employees) with cloud-native infrastructure on AWS/GCP
desired_outcomes: Reduce MTTR by 50%, consolidate from 3+ monitoring tools to one platform, achieve full-stack observability within 90 days
playbook_md:
# Onboarding Playbook: Datadog for Series B SaaS

## Overview
This playbook guides Series B SaaS companies through adopting Datadog as their unified observability platform. The goal is full-stack visibility within 90 days, replacing fragmented monitoring tools with a single pane of glass.

## Timeline & Milestones

| Week | Milestone | Owner | Success Criteria |
|------|-----------|-------|------------------|
| 1 | Kickoff & agent deployment | CSM + Engineering Lead | Datadog agent installed on 100% of production hosts |
| 2 | Infrastructure monitoring live | Engineering Lead | All hosts, containers, and services reporting metrics |
| 3 | APM instrumentation | Engineering Lead | Top 10 critical services instrumented with traces |
| 4 | Log pipeline setup | Engineering Lead | Centralized log collection with parsing rules |
| 5-6 | Dashboard buildout | CSM + Eng Lead | Executive dashboard + per-team service dashboards |
| 7-8 | Alert tuning | On-call team | SLO-based alerts replacing legacy threshold alerts |
| 9-10 | Legacy tool deprecation | Engineering Manager | Old monitoring tools decommissioned |
| 11-12 | Optimization & handoff | CSM | Usage optimization, cost review, handoff to ongoing CS |

## Kickoff & Welcome

### Pre-Kickoff Checklist
- [ ] Technical POC identified (usually Staff/Senior SRE)
- [ ] Executive sponsor confirmed (VP Engineering or CTO)
- [ ] Current monitoring tool inventory documented
- [ ] AWS/GCP account access for integration setup
- [ ] Datadog org provisioned with correct plan tier

### Kickoff Meeting Agenda (60 min)
1. **Introductions & goals** (10 min) — Align on the 90-day success metrics
2. **Current state review** (15 min) — Existing tools, pain points, gaps
3. **Architecture walkthrough** (15 min) — Their infrastructure topology and key services
4. **Deployment plan** (15 min) — Agent rollout strategy, instrumentation plan
5. **Communication & cadence** (5 min) — Slack channel, weekly syncs, escalation path

## Email/Touchpoint Sequence

### Day 1: Welcome Email
Subject: Welcome to Datadog — your 90-day onboarding plan
Content: Attach the timeline, introduce CSM and support contacts, link to getting-started docs.

### Day 3: Technical Kickoff Reminder
Subject: Technical kickoff this week — prep checklist
Content: Share the pre-kickoff checklist, request infrastructure diagram, confirm attendees.

### Week 2: Progress Check
Subject: Week 2 check-in — infrastructure monitoring milestone
Content: Confirm agent deployment coverage, share early wins (first dashboard screenshots), address any deployment blockers.

### Week 4: Mid-Point Review
Subject: Halfway there — APM + Logs milestone review
Content: Review instrumentation coverage, share MTTR improvement data from first month, plan dashboard buildout.

### Week 8: Alert Tuning Workshop
Subject: Alert tuning workshop — replacing legacy alerts
Content: Schedule a working session to migrate from threshold-based to SLO-based alerting. Share best practices doc.

### Week 12: Success Review & Handoff
Subject: 90-day review — your Datadog success metrics
Content: Present before/after metrics, ROI summary, introduce ongoing CS contact, schedule QBR.

## Check-in Cadence

| Cadence | Format | Attendees | Purpose |
|---------|--------|-----------|--------|
| Weekly (Weeks 1-6) | 30-min video call | CSM + Technical POC | Unblock deployment issues, track milestones |
| Bi-weekly (Weeks 7-12) | 30-min video call | CSM + Technical POC | Alert tuning, optimization, legacy deprecation |
| Monthly (Post-onboarding) | 30-min video call | CSM + Exec Sponsor | Usage review, expansion opportunities, QBR prep |

## Success Criteria & Health Signals

### Green (On Track)
- Agent deployed on 90%+ of hosts by Week 2
- At least 5 critical services instrumented with APM by Week 4
- First SLO-based alert created by Week 6
- MTTR improvement measurable by Week 8

### Yellow (Needs Attention)
- Agent deployment below 70% by Week 2
- APM instrumentation delayed beyond Week 5
- No dashboards created by Week 6
- Executive sponsor disengaged

### Red (At Risk)
- Agent deployment stalled below 50%
- Technical POC reassigned or unavailable
- No progress on legacy tool deprecation by Week 8
- Competing initiative deprioritizing the rollout

## Handoff to Ongoing CS

### Handoff Checklist
- [ ] 90-day success metrics documented and shared with exec sponsor
- [ ] All legacy monitoring tools decommissioned (or deprecation plan in place)
- [ ] Ongoing CS contact introduced and briefed
- [ ] QBR scheduled for Month 4
- [ ] Expansion opportunities identified (security, CI/CD, synthetics)
- [ ] Customer reference potential assessed

product_desc: Connected workspace for docs, wikis, project management, and knowledge management with AI-powered features
customer_segment: Mid-market companies (200-1000 employees) replacing legacy wiki and project management tools
desired_outcomes: 80% team adoption within 60 days, successful migration from Confluence, establish Notion as single source of truth for documentation
playbook_md:
# Onboarding Playbook: Notion for Mid-Market Teams

## Overview
This playbook guides mid-market companies through migrating from legacy tools (Confluence, Google Docs, Asana) to Notion as their unified workspace. The goal is 80% team adoption within 60 days with Notion established as the single source of truth.

## Timeline & Milestones

| Week | Milestone | Owner | Success Criteria |
|------|-----------|-------|------------------|
| 1 | Kickoff & workspace setup | CSM + IT Admin | Workspace provisioned, SSO configured, admin trained |
| 2 | Pilot team onboarding | CSM + Pilot Team Lead | First team (usually Product or Engineering) fully onboarded |
| 3 | Content migration (Phase 1) | IT Admin + CSM | Critical docs migrated from Confluence, templates created |
| 4 | Team template rollout | CSM + Department Leads | Standardized templates for meetings, PRDs, sprint planning |
| 5-6 | Company-wide rollout | CSM + IT Admin | All teams onboarded, SCIM provisioning active |
| 7-8 | Adoption optimization | CSM | Usage analytics review, power user identification, training gaps |

## Kickoff & Welcome

### Pre-Kickoff Checklist
- [ ] IT Admin identified for workspace management
- [ ] Executive sponsor confirmed (usually VP of Operations or CTO)
- [ ] Pilot team selected (recommend Product or Engineering — most natural fit)
- [ ] SSO provider confirmed (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace)
- [ ] Content migration inventory started (top 50 most-accessed Confluence pages)

### Kickoff Meeting Agenda (45 min)
1. **Goals & success metrics** (10 min) — Align on 60-day adoption targets
2. **Workspace architecture** (15 min) — Teamspace structure, permission model
3. **Migration plan** (10 min) — Priority content, migration approach, timeline
4. **Pilot team plan** (10 min) — First team, training approach, feedback loops

## Email/Touchpoint Sequence

### Day 1: Welcome Email
Subject: Welcome to Notion — your 60-day onboarding plan
Content: Timeline overview, CSM intro, link to workspace setup guide, admin training video.

### Week 2: Pilot Team Launch
Subject: Pilot team launch — training resources inside
Content: Share pilot team training materials, template library, FAQ, and feedback form.

### Week 4: Migration Progress Check
Subject: Migration check-in — are your critical docs moved?
Content: Review migration progress, share adoption metrics from pilot team, plan company-wide rollout.

### Week 6: Company-Wide Launch
Subject: Notion is live for everyone — launch resources
Content: All-hands training video, department-specific templates, Notion tips Slack channel.

### Week 8: Success Review
Subject: 60-day review — your Notion adoption metrics
Content: Adoption dashboard, power user highlights, next steps for advanced features (AI, API).

## Check-in Cadence

| Cadence | Format | Attendees | Purpose |
|---------|--------|-----------|--------|
| Weekly (Weeks 1-4) | 30-min video call | CSM + IT Admin + Pilot Lead | Unblock migration, track pilot adoption |
| Weekly (Weeks 5-8) | 30-min video call | CSM + IT Admin | Company-wide rollout progress, training gaps |
| Monthly (Post-onboarding) | 30-min video call | CSM + Exec Sponsor | Usage analytics, expansion (AI, API), QBR prep |

## Success Criteria & Health Signals

### Green (On Track)
- Pilot team at 90%+ weekly active usage by Week 3
- 50+ critical docs migrated by Week 4
- Company-wide rollout on schedule at Week 5
- 80%+ overall adoption by Week 8

### Yellow (Needs Attention)
- Pilot team adoption below 70% at Week 3
- Migration falling behind (fewer than 30 docs by Week 4)
- Resistance from specific departments
- Executive sponsor not actively championing

### Red (At Risk)
- Pilot team abandoning Notion for old tools
- Migration stalled — team reverting to Confluence
- No executive communication about the transition
- SSO/provisioning blockers unresolved past Week 2

## Handoff to Ongoing CS

### Handoff Checklist
- [ ] 60-day adoption metrics documented and shared
- [ ] Confluence fully deprecated (or deprecation timeline set)
- [ ] Ongoing CS contact introduced
- [ ] QBR scheduled for Month 3
- [ ] Advanced feature opportunities identified (Notion AI, API integrations, automations)
- [ ] Power users identified for Notion Champions program
